source.title: Reactivation of ocular dominance plasticity in the adult visual cortex.

confidence_rationale: Direct causal demonstration that critical-period closure is an ACTIVE, maintained molecular lock rather than passive plasticity decay: perineuronal-net / CSPG maturation coincides with closure, is delayed by dark rearing, and enzymatic degradation (chondroitinase-ABC) in adults restores ocular-dominance plasticity. This is the core of MECH-334's 'closure is active locking, reversible in principle'. Slightly below the anchor's 0.92 to reserve headroom for the substrate-transfer gap.
failure_signatures: Plasticity reactivation required active enzymatic removal of the lock; spontaneous reopening did not occur -- MECH-334's closure must therefore be modelled as a maintained constraint, not a one-time event, or the crystallized distribution will not in fact be protected long-term.

Closure timing tracked perineuronal-net assembly and was delayed (not abolished) by dark rearing -- closure is experience-modulated, so a MECH-334 implementation with a fixed closure epoch independent of the realised learning trajectory would diverge from the biology.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Maturation of chondroitin-sulphate-proteoglycan perineuronal nets coincides with the end of the visual-cortex critical period and is delayed by dark rearing; degrading these nets with chondroitinase-ABC in adult rats restores monocular-deprivation-induced ocular-dominance plasticity -- the mature extracellular matrix is actively inhibitory for experience-dependent plasticity.
mapping.ree_translation: Pizzorusso et al. evidence MECH-334's central assertion that closure is ACTIVE LOCKING, not passive reduction: a maintained structural constraint that protects the crystallized distribution and can, in principle, be lifted. This is the biology behind REE's design choice that post-window the diversity distribution is write-protected while dominant-pathway update scale is restored.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The lock is a physical/biochemical structure (perineuronal nets); REE's analogue is an algorithmic write-protection (EWC-style penalty on crystallized diversity weights). The transfer is to the abstract property 'closure is an actively maintained, in-principle-reversible constraint', not to extracellular-matrix biology. Evidences MECH-334 only; INV-074's necessity is upstream.
mapping.source_context: Rats; perineuronal-net histology; chondroitinase-ABC injection; monocular deprivation; ocular-dominance and visual-acuity readouts in adult visual cortex.

confidence_components.notes: Landmark Science paper, frequently replicated. Mapping fidelity high for the active-locking property MECH-334 asserts; trimmed by the structural-lock vs algorithmic-write-protection abstraction. Closure-is-maintained and closure-is-experience-modulated logged as failure signatures because they constrain how MECH-334 must be implemented.
